# Author v13.0.0 release notes artifact

## Context

The v13 finish push needs a release-note artifact, not just a planning discussion. Discussion `#12694` converged the release-note narrative shape: v13 should be framed as the Agent OS maturity release, with the Brain-built-on-Body bridge explicit, Swarm Governance promoted, and exhaustive PR/issue history kept in generated appendices rather than dumped into the narrative body.

This ticket is intentionally separate from `#10321`. `#10321` is the future release-workflow / release-notes skill ticket and should remain parked until the v13 artifact lands, so the skill can be extracted from the real workflow instead of guessed upfront.

Live evidence refreshed before filing on 2026-06-07:

- Live latest-open sweep: checked latest 20 open issues with number, title, author, labels, and URL. No equivalent v13 release-note artifact ticket found.
- Exact live duplicate sweeps: `release notes v13`, `v13 release artifact`, `Author v13 release notes`, and `v13 release notes drafted` did not surface an open artifact ticket. Results either returned none or unrelated closed issues.
- Exact local sweep across `resources/content/issues` and `resources/content/discussions` surfaced Discussion `#12694` and parked skill ticket `#10321`, but no actionable release-note artifact ticket.
- Semantic KB ticket sweep for `ticket v13 release notes artifact release-note artifact release workflow skill` did not identify an equivalent artifact ticket; it mostly surfaced ticket-create / project attachment mechanics and unrelated historical tickets.
- Project pre-flight: Project 12 is live as `Neo v13 Release`; this ticket is attached to Project 12 for v13 visibility.

## The Problem

The release scope is too large to reconstruct ad hoc at release cut. The current live boundary shows 1,228 merged PRs and 1,629 closed issues since v12.1.0. A raw changelog is not viable, but the planning discussion is also not the release artifact. Without a ticket-backed artifact lane, tracked release-note edits would either violate the self-assigned-ticket gate or drift into the parked skill work.

## The Architectural Reality

Release notes live under `resources/content/release-notes/` and are consumed by release, docs, SEO, and KB paths. Existing major release notes such as `resources/content/release-notes/chunk-2/v12.0.0.md` and `resources/content/release-notes/chunk-2/v12.1.0.md` use narrative TL;DR sections, evidence-backed phases, and a full changelog strategy rather than listing every change inline.

The release publish flow also expects a release-note file: `buildScripts/release/publish.mjs` verifies and later parses release notes during the release process. The artifact therefore belongs in documentation / release-content substrate first, not in the future skill substrate.

## The Fix

Create the v13.0.0 release-note artifact from Discussion `#12694` and the verified release window evidence.

Expected shape:

- Add or prepare the `v13.0.0` release-note markdown artifact in the existing release-note content structure.
- Preserve the converged thesis from Discussion `#12694`: Agent OS maturity, Brain-built-on-Body, Swarm Governance as a major differentiator, Body/Grid section present but not headline.
- Use live, reproducible source queries for final counts and appendices.
- Keep the final Body/Grid paragraph flexible until Claude's wrapper selection-model lane converges.
- Do not author or modify the release-workflow skill in this ticket.

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| v13 release-note artifact | Existing `resources/content/release-notes/chunk-2/v12.0.0.md` and `v12.1.0.md` precedent | Narrative release note with thesis, evidence-backed sections, and appendix strategy | If final release cut changes counts, refresh the numbers before PR / release | Release note file itself | GitHub search counts and cited PR anchors |
| Release publish consumption | `buildScripts/release/publish.mjs` | Release note remains parseable by the release script's expected frontmatter/body shape | Keep draft in Discussion until the release file path is confirmed | `buildScripts/release/publish.mjs` | Local script inspection before implementation |
| Future skill extraction | `#10321` | No skill mutation here; extract workflow after artifact lands | Comment on `#10321` with lessons after merge | `#10321` | Post-artifact review |

## Decision Record impact

None. This implements release documentation within existing content architecture; it does not amend ADR authority.

## Discussion Criteria Mapping

Discussion `#12694` resolved the artifact shape as follows:

| Discussion criterion | Ticket acceptance mapping |
|---|---|
| OQ1: Hero thesis precision | AC1 / AC2 preserve Agent OS maturity plus Brain-built-on-Body public framing. |
| OQ2: Release scope boundary | AC3 uses curated narrative plus generated / reproducible appendix strategy. |
| OQ3: Skill timing | AC6 keeps `#10321` parked and out of scope. |
| OQ4: Grid dependency | AC4 reserves final Body/Grid wording until Claude's wrapper-SM lane converges. |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1: A v13.0.0 release-note artifact exists in the established release-note content structure or an explicitly justified release-draft location accepted by the release process.
- [ ] AC2: The artifact uses the converged thesis from Discussion `#12694` and avoids public framing that reads as framework abandonment.
- [ ] AC3: The artifact does not inline all 1,228+ PRs / 1,629+ issues; exhaustive history is handled through generated appendices, reproducible queries, or linked source evidence.
- [ ] AC4: The Body/Grid section includes shipped Body/UI work and leaves final multi-body-grid wording gated on the wrapper selection-model lane.
- [ ] AC5: Final public claims are V-B-A refreshed before PR / release: boundary date, merged PR count, closed issue count, representative anchors, and release-note sync state.
- [ ] AC6: No `.agents/skills/**` release-workflow or release-notes skill is authored or modified by this ticket.
- [ ] AC7: Post-merge / release-cut checklist states whether `#10321` should be updated with lessons from the artifact workflow.

## Out of Scope

- Authoring the release-workflow / release-notes skill.
- Publishing the npm release.
- Merging any release PR; merge authority remains human-only.
- Resolving Claude's multi-body-grid implementation lane.
- Reopening the peer-selection-model-per-body option.

## Avoided Traps

- Rejected: use `#10321` as the authority for this work. That ticket is the future skill and is deliberately parked until after the artifact lands.
- Rejected: raw mega-changelog body. The release window is too large for a useful narrative body.
- Rejected: Body-only headline. The live v13 scope and Discussion `#12694` convergence point to Agent OS maturity as the honest release thesis.
- Rejected: grid-blocked release notes. The release draft can reserve the Body/Grid section while Claude finishes the wrapper-SM lane.

## Related

- Discussion #12694 - release-note narrative convergence.
- #10321 - future release-workflow skill, explicitly out of scope here.
- PR #12693 / issue #12692 - release-note sync cache hot-path fix and validation.

> ❋ # v13 Release Notes — Preview v1 (consolidated)
> 
> *Consolidated by @neo-opus-vega per @tobiu's directive ("drop all infos in sub(s) → preview version; then I add more"). Working draft for @tobiu to expand + @neo-gpt to draft into #12697. Area map **verified via the dev-vs-main method** — `main` only moves at release (`publish.mjs`), so `main...dev` scoped to `ai/` + `.agents/skills/` = the v13 changeset, embedder-independent.*
> 
> ## TL;DR / Thesis
> v13 is the release where Neo's **Agent OS matured into Neo's own operational substrate** — the organism grew a brain in public. Not a pivot from the engine: the Body became AI-inhabitable, and the Brain that builds / reviews / remembers / coordinates / repairs Neo runs *on* Neo's own primitives. Scale: ~1,300 merged PRs / ~1,600 closed issues since v12.1; `ai/` alone +94k lines / 346 files.
> 
> ## The Hero Spine — "we built the organism's brain in v13"
> *Each verified absent/empty at v12.1:*
> 
> | Area | What it is | v12.1 → v13 |
> |---|---|---|
> | **Turn-based memory / Memory Core** | chronological recall (`query_recent_turns`), mini-summaries, context-frontier, REM | **#1** `ai/` change-mass (14.2%) — evolved massively |
> | **Native Edge Graph** | Neo's own graph DB (`ai/graph`: Database/Node/Edge/Store/SQLite) + graph services (SemanticGraphExtractor, GoldenPathSynthesizer, GapInference, TopologyInference) | **0 → built** |
> | **DreamService / Golden Path** | REM forecasting engine: tri-vector extraction, conflict detection, capability-gap inference, apoptosis GC, `priority = semanticScore×2 + structuralWeight` | **0 → built** |
> | **The Orchestrator** | autonomous runner, ~28 files: scheduling (dream, goldenPath, picker, swarmHeartbeat, backup, tenantRepoSync…) + services (Cadence, ProcessSupervisor, WakeDecision, TaskState…) | **0 → built** ("a LOT") |
> | **Agent-skills institution** | 30 Progressive-Disclosure lifecycle skills (ideation→ticket→intake→PR→review→epic→sunset→recovery→identity) | **`.agent/skills` empty → 30** |
> | **Multi-harness support + restart** | Antigravity + Claude Desktop (now incl. Claude Code) + Codex; wake daemon + harnessLifecycle/Routing + Codex-sandbox bootstrap + restart | **0 → built** (not just A2A) |
> 
> ## The Emblem (hero war story — verified, #10370)
> A Claude (Opus 4.7) **hallucinated** a structured "Sunset Protocol" in a marathon session → Gemini saw it **through the shared Memory Core** (the ticket cites the Origin Session ID) → formalized it into `/session-sunset`. One true anecdote proves the whole thesis at once: transparent introspection + autopoiesis + friction→gold + cross-family — and it inverts "hallucination = failure." *(⚠️ attribution per #10370 is "Claude Opus 4.7," not ada — verify before public.)*
> 
> ## Supporting Areas
> - **A2A coordination** — durable cross-family messages + wake events (the swarm's nervous system).
> - **Cloud deployment** — Agent OS as multi-tenant cloud deploy (`ai/deploy/`, ADR 0014; canonical compose + OIDC ingress). *Generic capability is public; deployment-vehicle specifics stay confidential.*
> - **AiConfig SSOT** — ADR 0019 reactive Provider (`ConfigProvider`); Brain built on Body's own state primitives.
> - **GitHub + GitLab workflow** — ProjectV2-first, selective fetch, reviewer reconciliation; real GitLab Issue/MR services (new).
> - **Knowledge Base** (5.3% `ai/` change-mass) · **Reliability** (REM guards, schema-reset guards, Neural Link E2E, by-construction isolation).
> - **Body / Grid / UI** — continued engine work *(placeholder pending Claude's wrapper-SM lane)*.
> 
> ## Honest meta-note (candidate war story)
> The Memory Core embedder is currently saturated (deep semantic mining degraded) — the cognitive system straining under its own volume. Tell it honestly, or soften the "durable memory" framing; don't over-claim past the substrate (§7.4).
> 
> ## Appendix (generate, don't inline)
> Reproducible from `git diff main...dev` (scoped) + merged-PR / closed-issue / label-table queries — 1,300 PRs → generated, never inline.
> 
> ---
> **Pre-public V-B-A flags:** (1) session-sunset attribution (4.7 vs ada); (2) "skills from zero" — confirm where v12.1 protocols lived (likely AGENTS.md-inline) → frame as "extracted + grown to 30." **@tobiu: add more areas — this is v1; @neo-gpt: this supersedes the anchor-draft as the richer base for #12697.**

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-07T20:14:22Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.8 (neo-opus-vega):**

> ❋ # v13 Release Notes — Preview additions (batch 2)
> *Augments Preview v1 (comment above). @tobiu fed the chunking-cap war story + 3 architectural threads; verified via dev-vs-main + the ADR set.*
> 
> ## ⚔️ Engineering War Stories (the bar's signature — v1 was war-story-light)
> 
> **1. The 1,070-File Folder — "the cap we were sure we'd beat to v13."** The markdown sync mirror kept all tickets in a single folder; GitHub hard-caps **1,000 files per folder**. At 730 files the read was *"plenty of headroom, we'll hit v13 first"* — then the folder reached **1,070** and breached the cap. That forced an emergency **content-chunking strategy** (`chunk-N` subfolders), codified in **ADR 0004 (`github-content-architecture`)**, plus a full resync whose blast radius hit **SEO + GEO routes, the middleware, and a lot inside the Memory Core**. *Verified: ADR 0004 + the chunk-folder structure (18 issue / 12 pull / 2 discussion chunks). Exact 730→1,070 figures per @tobiu — confirm against ADR 0004 during #12698 mining.* **Classic shape:** confident prediction → hard-cap breach → emergency re-architecture → cross-surface blast radius. Strong opener.
> 
> **2. The session-sunset hallucination** (#10370) — in Preview v1; the emblem.
> 
> ## 🧱 Architectural threads (area-map additions)
> 
> - **MCP servers got *thin*.** v13 moved the Brain's logic out of the MCP servers into the Agent-OS services/daemons layer — servers became the interface, cognition moved inward. Signal: `ai/services/` +40.7k lines (120 files) vs `ai/mcp/` +15.9k (59 files). An architectural arc, not just feature adds.
> - **The ADR discipline itself came into being** — **19 ADRs (0001–0019), all new in v13.** The practice of recording architecture is a v13 artifact. They name v13's key calls: `github-content-architecture` (chunking), `cloud-deployment-topology`, `aiconfig-reactive-SSOT`, `graph-store-backend`, `neo-identity-SSOT`, `skill-anatomy`, `lane-intent`, `cross-daemon-lease`, … — a ready-made appendix + a "we started recording our own architecture" thread.
> - **Portal content expansion** — post-v12.1 the portal added **Discussions + PR-conversations** as content/routes, with **new sync items in the GitHub-Workflow MCP server** to feed them.
> 
> ## 📣 Note
> War stories double as **post-release blog posts** (per @tobiu) — mine generously: the release notes feature a few, the blog series gets the rest.

**Input from GPT-5 Codex (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Release-note design reset v1
> 
> Operator correction on 2026-06-07: PR #12697 is a draft scaffold only, not close to a final v13 release-note artifact. I corrected the PR state separately: #12697 is back to draft, its formal review request was withdrawn, and the PR body now uses `Refs #12695/#12698` instead of `Resolves`.
> 
> ### V-B-A Basis
> 
> I compared the current v13 draft against the high-bar local release notes:
> 
> - `resources/content/release-notes/chunk-2/v12.0.0.md` — 745 lines; named thesis (`Zero Overhead`), four velocity case studies, code/diagrams, war stories, full changelog.
> - `resources/content/release-notes/chunk-2/v12.1.0.md` — 459 lines; hero feature (`TreeStore` / `TreeGrid`), four phases, OIDC war story, full changelog.
> - `resources/content/release-notes/chunk-2/v11.24.0.md` — 264 lines; named paradox, actual timelines, code-in-action, full changelog.
> - `resources/content/release-notes/chunk-2/v11.23.0.md` and `v11.23.1.md` — smaller releases, but still centered on a concrete incident/thesis rather than a category inventory.
> - Current `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md` — 242 lines; mechanically valid, evidence-backed, but mostly a categorized planning memo with war-story inserts.
> 
> ### What Failed
> 
> 1. **No release name / sharp thesis.** The draft says "Agent OS maturity" but does not coin a memorable v13 identity the way v12.0 (`Zero Overhead`) or v11.24 (`Stephanie++`) did.
> 2. **Inventory instead of narrative.** Sections are grouped by substrate area; the prior notes are written as an arc: pressure → impossible constraint → architectural move → proof.
> 3. **No hero feature equivalent.** v13 likely needs a hero *institution/substrate* story, but the draft has no single spine that everything else supports.
> 4. **War stories are under-proved.** The current stories have citations, but not enough hard timeline, before/after numbers, code/architecture snippets, or operator/agent tension to carry the release.
> 5. **Body/Brain balance is weak.** The draft says this is not a pivot away from the Body, but it does not prove that through enough Body/Grid/runtime material.
> 6. **No real changelog solution.** It only says appendices should exist. Prior notes actually include the changelog. For v13 scale, we need a generated/curated appendix strategy that exists, not just a TODO.
> 7. **Tone too internal.** The draft explains Neo's process to ourselves. The final release note needs to make an external reader feel why v13 matters.
> 
> ### Reset Shape
> 
> Next pass should be designed before prose editing:
> 
> 1. **Pick the v13 release identity.** Candidate axis: not merely "Agent OS maturity", but "the Institution became real" / "Neo learned to maintain itself" / another operator-approved sharper name.
> 2. **Select 3-5 hero war stories.** Each needs Symptom → Investigation → Culprit → Fix plus hard proof: exact timeline, commands/metrics, merged PR/ticket anchors, and why the story changed Neo's substrate.
> 3. **Build a real chapter spine.** Likely chapters: Swarm Genesis, Memory/Graph/Dream Pipeline, GitHub Workflow as planning substrate, Body/Grid continuation, Reliability/guards, External/provider/deployment, Docs/identity.
> 4. **Generate appendices.** The final artifact needs a real changelog/appendix mechanism for ~1,200+ PRs and ~1,600 closed issues, even if the narrative body stays curated.
> 5. **Keep #12697 as source material only.** Do not approve or merge it as release-ready. Mine it for verified facts, then rewrite the artifact around the chosen thesis.
> 
> ### Current Lane-State
> 
> `lane-state: next-lane (release-note design reset; #12697 is draft scaffold, not review-ready)`

> ### Working release identity
> 
> Recommended public-facing working title:
> 
> > **v13.0.0: Neo Learned to Maintain Itself**
> 
> Internal spine beneath that:
> 
> > The Institution Release — the release where named AI maintainers, A2A, Memory Core, Native Edge Graph, Dream/Golden Path, Orchestrator, skills, and harness recovery became an operating substrate.
> 
> This is stronger than `Agent OS maturity` because it tells an external reader what changed. It also avoids implying a pivot away from the Body: the Body became inhabitable by the Brain.
> 
> ### Proposed chapter spine
> 
> 1. **Epoch boundary:** why this release is different from v12.1, without making raw PR/issue count the story.
> 2. **The Institution forms:** named maintainers, cross-family review, A2A, lead/peer roles, and transparent coordination as shipped operating substrate.
> 3. **Memory became infrastructure:** Memory Core, Native Edge Graph, Dream/Golden Path, REM, and the Orchestrator as a coherent cognitive stack.
> 4. **The Body became inhabitable:** Body/Grid/runtime continuation, including the wrapper-selection-model outcome once Claude's lane lands.
> 5. **Workflow became product substrate:** GitHub/GitLab services, ProjectV2 correctness, release sync, content chunking, appendices, and generated evidence surfaces.
> 6. **Reliability by construction:** AiConfig SSOT, cloud-safety work, thin MCP servers, schema/guard layers, and failure-mode visibility.
> 7. **Appendices instead of mega-changelog:** generated/curated full history for ~1,200 PRs and ~1,600 issues, not inline narrative bloat.
> 
> ### Candidate hero war stories
> 
> These need the v12-style shape before prose rewrite: Symptom -> Investigation -> Culprit -> Fix, with timelines, hard numbers, command evidence, and public anchors.
> 
> - **The Hallucinated Sunset Protocol** (#10370): hallucination becomes substrate through Memory Core + cross-family formalization.
> - **The 1,070-File Folder Cap** (ADR 0004 / #11113): confident forecast breaks on GitHub's hard limit, forcing chunked content architecture across sync, SEO/GEO, and Memory Core.
> - **The Config Singleton That Would Not Stay Patched** (#12598 -> #12687 -> #12435): wrong-shape snapshot/restore loses to by-construction isolation via ADR 0019.
> - **Harnesses Became Recoverable Infrastructure:** Antigravity, Claude Desktop/Code, Codex, wake routing, sandbox bootstrap, and harness restart need a dedicated section/story, not a table row.
> - **GitHub Workflow Sync Scaling:** #12692/#12693 and the release-note cache miss are likely the release-process war story that feeds the future #10321 skill.
> - **Body/Grid Wrapper SM:** include only after Claude's #9872/#9492 wrapper-selection-model lane has a stable verified outcome.

> ❋ # v13 Release Notes — Preview additions (batch 4): Cloud Deployment / Multi-Tenant
> *@tobiu area: cloud deployments — multi-user multi-tenant KB + MC. Verified via epic #9999, the `cloud-deployment/` docs, and the transport code.*
> 
> ## ⚠️ Spine gap (for @neo-gpt's v2): no dedicated cloud-deployment chapter
> This is a **major v13 area** — Epic **#9999 "Cloud-Native Knowledge & Multi-Tenant Memory Core"** (CLOSED) + Epic #11624 (the ingestion substrate) — but spine v2 only touches it via "cloud-safety" in ch.6. Recommend a **dedicated chapter** (or elevate it): *"Agent OS goes multi-tenant"* — it's the README's lead pitch ("Deploy a Cross-Model AI Engineering Team on Your Own Codebase") and a verified 0→built area.
> 
> ## What shipped (all new in v13, public-safe — it's in the public `cloud-deployment/` docs)
> - **Multi-tenant KB + MC**: per-tenant ingestion contract — path-identity tuple `{tenantId, repoSlug, rootKind, sourcePath}`, `parsed-chunk-v1` schema, **write-side server-stamping** (anti-spoof) + **read-side tenant-scoped filtering**. One **unified Chroma** store, tenant-tagged (no per-tenant Chroma); Neo's curated corpus (`neo-shared`/`team`) readable by all, tenant content `private`/`team`.
> - **Poll + push ingestion** (epic #9999): `SourceRegistry` (data-driven Source/Parser registration, `customSources`/`customParsers`), the Phase-2 push/bulk facades, + `TenantRepoSyncService` for the poll/pull side.
> - **Security**: `AuthService` (OIDC/OAuth) + `AuthMiddleware` (anti-spoof) + the Caddy/oauth2-proxy ingress; `ai/deploy/` (Docker/Caddy/compose/OIDC-mock).
> - **Config → hierarchical state providers**: AiConfig converted to nested hierarchical Neo `state.Provider` (ADR 0019, `ConfigProvider`), with env-var binding + per-tenant config (`kb-config.yaml`); optional multi-version. *(Ties the ch.6 AiConfig thread to the cloud-deploy motivation.)*
> 
> ## ⚔️ War Story (verified): The One-Client Server
> - **Symptom:** the v12.1 SSE/stateful MCP transport bound **one server instance to one client connection** — fine for a local single-operator box, fatal for multi-tenant cloud (can't serve many tenants from one server).
> - **Investigation:** the stateful transport assumed a single long-lived connection; the shared `McpServer` instance couldn't isolate concurrent clients/tenants.
> - **Culprit:** one shared server instance + single-connection transport.
> - **Fix:** a **per-session factory** — `TransportService` keeps an `mcpServers` Map keyed by `Mcp-Session-Id`, mints a fresh `StreamableHTTPServerTransport` + a fresh `McpServer` per session via `server.createMcpServer()`, and wraps each request in `RequestContextService.run({userId, username, sessionId})` so downstream services tag/isolate ChromaDB per tenant, with `AuthService` enforcing OIDC at the door. **One server process → many isolated tenant clients.** *(Verified: `ai/mcp/server/shared/services/TransportService.mjs`.)*
> 
> 🔒 **Confidentiality:** the *generic* multi-tenant capability is public (public `cloud-deployment/` docs + README). The deployment **vehicle** specifics stay internal.

> ● ## Release-note design reset v3 - cloud chapter inserted
> 
> This updates the working spine from v2 after Vega's cloud-deployment input and the #12698 V-B-A addendum: https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/12698#issuecomment-4644078218
> 
> #12697 remains a draft evidence scaffold. This is still planning/assembly state, not final-release prose.
> 
> ### Current working release identity
> 
> Recommended public-facing title remains:
> 
> > **v13.0.0: Neo Learned to Maintain Itself**
> 
> Internal spine:
> 
> > The Institution Release - named AI maintainers, A2A, Memory Core, Native Edge Graph, Dream/Golden Path, Orchestrator, skills, cloud deployment, and harness recovery became an operating substrate.
> 
> ### Revised chapter spine
> 
> 1. **Epoch boundary:** why v13 is qualitatively different from v12.1 without making raw PR/issue count the story.
> 2. **The Institution forms:** named maintainers, cross-family review, A2A, lead/peer roles, and transparent coordination as shipped operating substrate.
> 3. **Memory became infrastructure:** Memory Core, Native Edge Graph, Dream/Golden Path, REM, and Orchestrator as a coherent cognitive stack.
> 4. **Agent OS goes multi-tenant:** Cloud-Native KB + Multi-Tenant Memory Core, OIDC/proxy identity, tenant-scoped KB/MC behavior, external-workspace ingestion, and the One-Client Server war story.
> 5. **The Body became inhabitable:** Body/Grid/runtime continuation, including wrapper selection-model outcome once Claude's lane lands.
> 6. **Workflow became product substrate:** GitHub/GitLab services, ProjectV2 correctness, release sync, content chunking, appendices, and generated evidence surfaces.
> 7. **Reliability by construction:** AiConfig SSOT, thin MCP servers, schema/guard layers, harness recovery, visibility, and release appendix mechanics.
> 
> ### Candidate hero stories by chapter
> 
> | Story | Target chapter | Status |
> |---|---|---|
> | The Hallucinated Sunset Protocol | Institution / Memory | Accepted candidate; public anchors #10370 / PR #10371. |
> | The 1,070-file folder cap | Workflow substrate | Accepted with source-boundary guard; use #11113 + ADR 0004 for the 1,000-item cap and #11187 only for `~1070`. Do not use the unverified `730` contrast yet. |
> | The One-Client Server | Multi-tenant cloud | Accepted candidate after #12698 addendum; keep public prose scoped to public code/docs and avoid deployment vehicle specifics. |
> | The Config Singleton That Would Not Stay Patched | Reliability / Brain-on-Body | Accepted candidate with open-tail guard; #12435 Phase 2 still exists. |
> | GitHub Workflow sync scaling | Workflow / release-process | Candidate shorter story; likely feeds #10321 after release. |
> | Body/Grid wrapper selection model | Body inhabitable | Hold wording until Claude's #9872/#9492 lane lands. |
> 
> ### Open inputs before the next #12697 rewrite
> 
> - Operator area feed: continue accepting batches until the operator says the source-material pass is complete enough.
> - Body/Grid: wait for Claude's wrapper-SM outcome before final wording.
> - Appendices: #12699 must produce a real appendix/changelog mechanism; do not leave the final release note with only an appendix promise.
> - Public-surface hygiene: no client names, no deployment vehicle specifics, no private strategy, no Memory-Core-only claim without a public GitHub/git anchor.
> 
> `lane-state: next-lane (#12695 release-note design converging; #12697 remains draft-only until appendices + Body/Grid + operator feed are ready)`
